ksa_text: Knowledge of cloud AI/ML platform services (e.g., SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML) and the architectural patterns for building portable versus cloud-native ML infrastructure.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.411) to justify inclusion for the Deputy CDO. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Deputy CDO roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm AI/ML Engineering is not a standard competency expectation for Deputy CDO. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Deputy CDO competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.411) and edge-domain placement (AI/ML Engineering) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Deputy CDO. All passes support exclusion.
